item.title: Site Criticism Team - Junior Staff Review
item.content:encoded:
Taking a quick look at this since Site News had it tagged as under +30.

+ Crit
- Crit

SCP-4913 is room 126 in the █████████ Hotel, Spokane Washington.

Should probably be "SCP-4913 is room 126 in the █████████ Hotel, located in Spokane Washington." or something to that effect.

no tests or experiments of any sort have been performed inside of it despite multiple attempts to do so.

Missing comma before "despite".

the Hume level inside of it

it -> the structure (or some other noun that can reference the hotel)

However, all of these attempts have failed for various and often improbable reasons.

No need for "however" here in my opinion.

SCP-4913 has one of three proposed anomalous effects:

Might I suggest using a numbered list instead of the current format? That is done the same way that bullet points are, but with the # symbol instead of the * symbol.

  1. they look
  2. like this

and are done

# like this

to determine if it really did have any anomalous properties.

The use of "really" here makes this sentence have rather poor clinical tone.

At this time the hotel was purchased by the Foundation and a permanent Foundation presence was created.

Needs a comma after "time".

paper-work

paperwork

possesses an antimemetic effect that makes successfully entering it impossible.

Antimemes don't have retrocausal effects like this. An antimeme would be unable to be perceived or otherwise experienced. 055, for example, is often just forgotten about entirely. What you are discussing would be something like a statistical anomaly.

Upon learning of this, Researcher Grooms theorized […]

Really, this whole bit reads very awkwardly, like an omniscient retelling of events. Not good, tonally speaking. Honestly a quick test log table would make this bit a lot easier to parse.

Senior Researcher █████

Bleh, you were doing just fine with blackboxes until right here. No reason to redact a Foundation doctor's name in a Foundation internal document. Civilian surnames and business names, sure, makes sense. Not doctors though. Not unless protecting their identity is important because of something they've done, for example.

Two Foundation personnel, Agent Arrowood and Agent Grey had been part of the security force

Need a comma after "Grey"

had been part of the security force assigned to the █████████ Hotel.
[…]
Agent Arrowood: There's something weird about this place.

Agent Grey: It's safe though, the Foundation has been guarding this hotel for years and nothing has happened.

Agent Arrowood: Then why are we here?

This exchange makes no sense given the above context. If they've been on the security team previous to this, why are they discussing the job like it's their first day? This pulls me out of the dialogue for sure.

Agent Grey: You okay, ████████?

Why is this redacted? Is it infohazardous, or is it just their first name? The blackboxes are ambiguous and convolute the meaning of what is going on here.

This is a strange one for me. I was mostly not enjoying myself at all for most of the article, until I sat back to think about the end of that interview, where it almost seems like both Grey and Arrowood tried to enter 4913, but all we see from our end are their "unsuccessful attempts". This fridge logic is great, and I wish the rest of the article could have been a slow build to this reveal, rather than the slog I found it to be.

This is gonna be a reluctant downvote from me, because the ending, no matter how much I liked it, really can't save an SCP I otherwise never would have bothered finishing if I wasn't trying to review it.

Disclaimer: Opinions expressed in this review do not reflect the opinions of SCP Wiki staff as a whole. Please respond to TheMightyMcB or contact the captain of the Site Criticism Team, SoullessSingularity, for any questions or concerns relating to this review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think an extended/amended test log could go a long way towards giving this its own unique flair.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't help but feel like they should check the door for a "Do Not Disturb" sign. Someone didn't want to be bothered and went overboard with the effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I do notice it being repetetive in certain parts in retrospect. Maybe I could add an extended test log?

Thanks for the feedback though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was imagining the Benny hill theme while reading this. I feel like the test log is too restrained, it would land much more solidly if it had some real bonkers attempts to get in this room with the added tension of dreading what could happen if they succeed. Right now it’s just the same information as the description in audio log form. I’m no-voting this but I like the start here.

Also those containment procedures are pathetic, I’m sure you can come up with something a little less generic than what’s here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You can still add that if you want, although I particularly think the idea hit the point already.

Maybe changing the animal test for a drone test, it seems to be more of a Foundation standard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It wouldn't have worked if they had (the drone would have broke or gotten lost) but that would have been a good idea. I missed that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My only question is why no one proposed sending an autonomous drone in there after the lock was picked?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you! Good luck on your own SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was a pleasure to help with the last revisions, and I liked the result, pretty much.

item.title: First SCP
item.content:encoded:
Hello everyone, this is my first SCP that I got actual feedback for (my first one was rushed for Jamcon which was a stupid idea in retrospect.)

Okay, so special thanks to MaliceAforethought, AlanDaris, Nameless Mediocre and Monteparnas for giving me some feedback. You guys are awesome!

And extra special thanks to UraniumEmpire, without whom this SCP would have crashed and burned as hard as my first (unreviewed) attempt did. She basically told me exactly what I needed to do to get this to work.

+ show block
– hide block

Anyway, I randomly thought of this random idea and wrote a rough draft before abandoning it. Weeks later I found the draft and liked it so I decided to make this for real.

If you're wondering what's happening in-universe… I won't tell. I have an idea that I think is correct but saying what it is will spoil the fun. I want this piece to be open to interpretation so everyone can view it whatever way works best for them.

Now, in the chat I heard a theory that this skip's real power is simply making people paranoid about it. While I can confirm that this is not what I was thinking when I wrote this, I will also say that it's a good guess.

There is no canon.

Beachfox's theory (see below) is great. I won't say that it's 100% true but… it could be!

And finally thanks to literally everyone who gives votes or feedback to this page! You guys are also awesome.

item.title: Pretty nice!
item.content:encoded: You have a great concept here, my friend.
